name: basejump
description: Convert and copy numeric values between bases (hex, decimal, binary, octal)

markdownEnumDescriptions: **Copy to Clipboard** — Selecting a conversion copies the converted value to the clipboard.
**Replace in Editor** — Selecting a conversion replaces the source token in the editor with the converted value. _(default)_
basejump.defaultAction.description: The default action when selecting a conversion from the QuickPick menu.

basejump.enableOctal.description: Enable or disable octal base in number detection and conversion.

basejump.enableBinaryNibbles.description: When converting to binary, also show a secondary result with nibble separators.

basejump.enableHexBytes.description: When converting to hexadecimal, also show a secondary result with byte (two hex digit) separators.

basejump.enableDecimalThousands.description: When converting to decimal, also show a secondary result with thousands separators (e.g. 1'000'000).

markdownEnumDescriptions:
**Apostrophe** `'` — The official digit separator in C++ (since C++14), Swift, Ruby, and several other languages. Ignored by the compiler.

Example: `0b1011'0101`  |  `0xFF'AA'BB`

**Underscore** `_` — Highly popular in programming languages (Python, Java, Rust) because the compiler ignores them, keeping code readable and functional.

Example: `0b1011_0101`  |  `0xFF_AA_BB`

**Space** — The most common and 'cleanest' way to separate nibbles in documentation or code comments.

Example: `0b1011 0101`

**Hyphen** `-` — Sometimes seen in hardware manuals or instructional materials to clearly segment the high and low nibbles.

Example: `0b1011-0101`

**Period** `.` — Occasionally used in networking or specific technical notations, though less common than the underscore in general programming.

Example: `0b1011.0101`
basejump.fallbackDelimiter.markdownDescription:
Fallback delimiter used to separate digit groups in Binary (nibbles), Hexadecimal (bytes), and Decimal (thousands) output. Only takes effect when no per-language override or built-in language default applies.

**Resolution order:**
1. Per-language override in your `settings.json` (e.g. `"[cpp]": { "basejump.fallbackDelimiter": "apostrophe" }`)
2. Built-in language defaults: C/C++ → `'`, Python/Rust/Java/Go/Kotlin/Swift → `_`, Markdown/plain text → space
3. This setting (global / workspace fallback)

scripts.pretest: yarn run compile && yarn run lint
